allies.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking helps einstellung-effect strip away the familiar template and rebuild the problem from facts instead of from habit.
allies.source_quote: First Principles Thinking explicitly counters the reliance on conventional, established methods.

allies.rationale_text: Step-back helps einstellung-effect interrupt the first locked-in path long enough to examine whether a different frame is needed.
allies.source_quote: The path to overcoming the effect is to subsequently turn the focus away from the subject.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias reinforces einstellung-effect by making teams selectively process evidence that protects the first familiar solution.
antagonists.source_quote: Confirmation bias reinforces the fixed path set by the initial solution.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Einstellung-effect conflicts with schema-acquisition when accumulated cognitive templates harden into fixed patterns that block fresh approaches to novel problems.
structured_tensions.source_quote: einstellung-effect vs schema-acquisition: einstellung-effect conflicts with schema-acquisition (schema acquisition) when accumulated cognitive templates harden into fixed patterns that block fresh approaches to novel problems.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved the clearest de-fixation allies plus the explicit template-hardening tension.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader outside-view and multidisciplinary-sprawl beyond the clearest current-vocabulary seams.
open_questions: Additional anti-fixation allies are source-real, but the first-order surface is cleanest around first-principles-thinking and step-back.
